## Release — backlog-campaign v0.16.0

### ✨ What's new in v0.16.0

Minor release with one **breaking** change: autonomy becomes the default and only
operating mode. Alongside it, a quality-enforcement batch adds four new review/planning
gates (design patterns, per-AC completion contracts, dependency blast-radius, and a
quick-track threat screen).

#### ⚠️ BREAKING — Autonomy is the default and only mode (ADR-014)

- **`autonomy.enabled` master switch removed.** Autonomy is now unconditionally on — the
  confidence-gate kernel is always active, and every former `autonomy.enabled &&
  autonomy.{sub_flag}` gate collapses to just `autonomy.{sub_flag}` across the router,
  orchestrator, planner, and the phase/queue/worker references.
- **Migration**: delete any `autonomy.enabled` key from `.blackhole/config.json` — it is no
  longer read. Campaigns that previously had no `autonomy` block (and therefore ran the older
  ask-on-everything categorical gates) now run the confidence-gated autonomous path by default.
- **Preserved**: the sub-flags remain as tuning knobs — `design_autonomy` and `analyze_routing`
  default on, **`brainstorm_routing` stays off by default** (terminal-closure semantics). The
  `never_bypass` floor (`destructive`, `credentials`, `epic-go-no-go`) is unchanged and always
  enforced — autonomy never bypasses those human gates.
- **Rollback**: there is no longer an `enabled: false` switch. Disable granularly via the
  sub-flags (`design_autonomy: false`, `analyze_routing: false`) or `adaptive_routing: false`,
  or revert the release.

#### Quality-enforcement batch

- **Design Pattern Review (`V-PAT-01..04`)** — God-object, circular-dependency, missing
  error-handling, and singleton/service-locator anti-pattern checks, wired into the reviewer and
  the refactor/best-practices hunt kinds.
- **Sprint Contract completion gate** — the implementer's Verification Evidence Gate now
  iterates each plan task's acceptance criterion at completion and emits a per-AC
  PASS/PARTIAL/N/A verdict (`sprint_contract_status` + `ac_results[]`, both optional and
  backward-compatible), with a completion-time hold when a criterion isn't satisfied.
- **Dependency Blast-Radius (`V-SCOPE-03`)** — Standard-track plans gain a conditional
  blast-radius section (triggered at 3+ affected consumers) reusing the existing grep-based
  consumer scan; the reviewer audits the estimate against actual diff scope.
- **Quick-track threat escalation (`V-THREAT-01`)** — a security-sensitive change on the Quick
  track now runs a plan-time threat screen (reusing the router's `security_review_required`
  signal) and escalates to the Standard track's full threat model before any code is written.

#### Docs

- **ADR-014** — autonomy as the default and only mode (removes ADR-010's opt-in invariant,
  completes ADR-012's deferred default flip).
- **Parity matrix + synergy assessment** — backlog sweep record and the autonomous-parity
  analysis under `documentation/audits/`.

---
